a:r.a:t: Prompt Architecture — How Prompts Are Built
query_loop.py _build_system_prompt() assembles 8 blocks in order. Skills and memory are injected dynamically. Fallback prompts cover 10 roles.

Loaded from data/agents/{name}.md via skill_runtime.build_prompt(). Contains mission statement, tool & delegation policy, capabilities/limits, anti-patterns. Falls back to prompt_fallbacks.py (181 lines) if file missing.
Clarification policy (static), pending clarifications (session metadata), resolved intent (turn metadata), active doc focus (session metadata). All optional — empty blocks are filtered out.
Injected when coordinator delegates via spawn_worker. Contains task_payload with instructions, expected output format, and artifact references.
SkillResolver matches query against skill packs via vector + BM25 scoring. Top matches injected as domain expertise. 11 packs today, scoped per agent.
User-scoped and conversation-scoped memories from PostgresMemoryStore. Selected by MemorySelector (deterministic or LLM mode). Capped at ~6 candidates.
Key gap: No reasoning protocol, self-reflection checkpoints, or confidence scoring injected into any block. Agents get missions and tools but no structured thinking instructions.
runtime/query_loop.py (line 405), prompt_fallbacks.py (181 lines), skills/runtime.py (176 lines), data/agents/*.md (11 files)
